sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to taste (as in all the cognate languages)-(a) to try the flavour, Job 12:11.-(b) to taste, to eat a little, 1Sa 14:24, 29 1Sa 14:29, 43 1Sa 14:43 Jon 3:7.-(c) to perceive by the taste or flavour, 2Sa 19:36. Metaph.

(2) to perceive mentally [or spiritually], Pro 31:18. Psa 34:9, טַעֲמוּ וּרְאוּ כִּי־טֹוב יְהֹוָה “taste (perceive) and see that Jehovah is good.”

Hence the words immediately following, and מַטְעַמִּים.
